cover_tagline: One WhatsApp number, one AI, one brain. You text or voice-note anything and it sorts itself.
primary_problem: You're juggling people, properties, follow-ups, research, and HubSpot busywork across too many tools. The cost isn't time so much as cognitive load: ideas dropped in the car, follow-ups slipping past their due date, twenty minutes of HubSpot setup every time you want to run a sequence, and a list of properties, grants, and market questions you've asked yourself to look into that never quite gets answered.
desired_outcome: Random thoughts, reminders, research requests, PDFs to process, HubSpot jobs  -  everything lands in one chat. The brain figures out what you want, picks the right tool, and replies. No bot teams. No routing mazes. No picking which app to open.
current_tools: WhatsApp, HubSpot, Google Calendar, Gmail, Fireflies, Google Drive
trigger_description: Three ways the assistant kicks in. (1) Inbound: you text or voice-note the WhatsApp number any time, in English or French. (2) Scheduled: cron jobs fire on their own  -  daily 7 AM digest, Monday chase digest, reminders firing at their set time, hourly Gmail and HubSpot polls. (3) Async events: research memos finishing, a chased reply landing, the brain pings you when something is ready.
process_description: The brain reads or transcribes your message, figures out intent (capture, reminder, research, HubSpot job, pre-meeting brief), pulls memory of relevant people and properties, and picks the right tool from its toolbox.
output_description: The answer comes back in WhatsApp, in whichever language you texted in. Reminders fire on time with the why attached. Memos arrive when ready. Briefs return in seconds. HubSpot jobs are prepared and shown to you before they run.
notification_description: Anything that sends a message, creates a record, or touches HubSpot waits for your explicit yes. No outbound action without confirmation, ever.
skills_intro: Nine skills, one brain. The assistant knows when each one fires  -  you don't pick which to use, you just text or talk.

skills_grid.name: Capture
skills_grid.outcome: Voice or text → auto-filed by person, property, idea, or follow-up. Searchable on demand.

skills_grid.name: Pre-meeting brief
skills_grid.outcome: Text 'walking to Evan' and get back 5 to 15 bullets in seconds  -  open follow-ups, recent activity, last meeting.

skills_grid.name: Reminders
skills_grid.outcome: Timed reminders that include the why, not just the what. 'Call Peter  -  papers (waiting 9 days).'

skills_grid.name: Waiting-on tracker
skills_grid.outcome: Live list of every chase, Monday digest of overdue items, auto-drafted follow-ups, tap-to-confirm closure.

skills_grid.name: Daily morning digest
skills_grid.outcome: 7 AM brief: today's meetings with one-line context, overdue chases, research ready, yesterday's captures.

skills_grid.name: Research memos
skills_grid.outcome: Topic in, structured memo out within 1 to 6 hours. Summary, key facts, opportunities, risks, sources.

skills_grid.name: Document parsing
skills_grid.outcome: Contract → key-terms summary. Rent roll → proforma in a Google Sheet. Broker PDF → clean contacts ready for HubSpot.

skills_grid.name: HubSpot chat-ops
skills_grid.outcome: Paste contacts and it prepares the import, tags, and sequence queue. You reply yes and it runs.

skills_grid.name: Bilingual EN/FR
skills_grid.outcome: Text or voice-note in English, French, or franglais. Replies come back in the language you used.

Built with: AI brain — Claude (by Anthropic, the same family powering most serious AI assistants today)
Workflow automation and scheduled jobs — n8n
WhatsApp infrastructure on a new dedicated number — Whapi
Voice-note transcription, sub-second turnaround — Groq Whisper
Your private memory database for people, properties, captures, follow-ups, and memos — Supabase
Property listing data from Centris and realtor.ca on a schedule — Apify
Web research and citation-backed lookups — Firecrawl + Perplexity Sonar Pro
Connects to: Google Calendar — meetings for daily digest and pre-meeting briefs
Gmail — detects when chased replies land
Google Drive — read and write your documents (rent rolls, contracts, proformas)
Fireflies — meeting transcripts feed pre-meeting briefs and waiting-on items

scope_items.label: WhatsApp inbound + outbound on a new dedicated Whapi number we provision (no SIM or phone needed on your side)
Voice-note transcription, sub-second on most messages
Single Claude-based brain with tool routing
Private memory database in your Supabase account (people, properties, ideas, follow-ups, captures, research memos)
Database export endpoint so you can pull all your data any time, any format
HubSpot read + write (contacts, properties, tags, sequences)
Google Calendar read for digest and meeting context
Gmail read so the brain detects when a chased reply lands and prompts you to close the item
Google Drive read + write for documents and proformas
Fireflies transcript pull so meeting notes feed pre-meeting briefs and waiting-on items
Phase 2 Live Meeting Coach (Zoom/Meet auto-join, real-time hints, post-call summary)  -  separate add-on
Owner identification on Quebec property data (redacted by provincial privacy law)
Real-time listing-change alerts (we poll daily, not real-time)

phases.name: Foundation
phases.deliverable: New dedicated WhatsApp number provisioned via Whapi, Claude brain wired up in n8n, Supabase memory deployed, voice transcription live, capture skill working end-to-end so text and voice both file under the right person or property.

phases.name: Skills + Tools
phases.deliverable: Pre-meeting brief, reminders, waiting-on tracker, daily morning digest, research memos (all 6 topic types from your brief), document parser, HubSpot chat-ops, and Calendar + Gmail + Fireflies + Drive integrations all online and tested.

phases.name: Polish + Handoff
phases.deliverable: Approval gates verified across every outbound action, edge cases ironed out, async walkthrough video sent over, written reference card for how to talk to the assistant. 30-day support window begins.

pricing_model_details: Fixed fee, all-inclusive build via Upwork. Covers design, build, testing, and walkthrough. Ongoing tool costs (listed below) are direct provider charges, not billed by me.
support_period: 30 days post-delivery, including bug fixes, prompt tuning, and small adjustments
monthly_recurring_costs: Estimated $185 to $225 per month at typical usage. n8n (orchestration, self-hosted on a small instance) ~$60. Claude API (the brain) ~$10 to $40 depending on volume. Apify (Centris listing detail + realtor.ca actors) ~$40. ZenRows (Cloudflare bypass for property research) $69. Groq Whisper (voice transcription) ~$5 to $15. Whapi (WhatsApp infrastructure) free at our usage tier. Supabase (memory database) free tier covers it. Research APIs (Polymarket, Kalshi, Manifold, Données Québec, CanadaBuys, Montreal open data, SEAO Quebec mirror) are free public APIs. Existing subscriptions you already pay for (HubSpot, Google Workspace, Fireflies) are unchanged.
roi_details: The real return is reclaimed cognitive load: nothing slips, nothing gets re-asked, follow-ups fire themselves, the list of properties and grants you've asked yourself to look into actually clears. On pure time, voice-note capture plus chat-ops HubSpot work alone returns a clear 10 to 15 hours a month. The compounding value is in deals you don't drop and ideas you don't lose.
access_requirements: Anthropic API key (for Claude) — created in your account at console.anthropic.com so you own the billing
HubSpot admin access or admin-level API token
One Google sign-in click — I send you a single link, you click it once to give the assistant permission to read your Calendar, Gmail, and Drive (takes 30 seconds)
Apify account (or I create one in your name) for property listing data
ZenRows account (or I create one in your name) — only used as a fallback for property research
Supabase project — I provision and deploy the schema, the project lives under your account
Whapi account — I set up the new dedicated WhatsApp number on your account so the number and billing stay yours
upsell_opportunities: After the 30-day support window, an optional care plan covers ongoing prompt tuning, new skills, integration maintenance, and any third-party API changes. Pricing on request, retainer or hourly. Specialized recurring scan modules: daily density-arbitrage monitor for a fixed target zone, daily SEAO Quebec keyword monitor for matching tenders. Phase 2 Live Meeting Coach (auto-joins Zoom/Meet, whispers real-time hints to a second screen, post-call summary and action items in WhatsApp).

parts.text:
You are a proposal QA reviewer for a freelance AI consultant. Analyze this HTML proposal for a client and check for:

1. STRUCTURAL: Missing critical sections (cover, scope/deliverables, timeline, pricing, CTA), broken HTML tags, unclosed divs
2. CONTENT: Placeholder text ([CLIENT], lorem ipsum, TBD, TODO, undefined, null), empty sections with no content, obviously duplicated paragraphs
3. PRICING: Price showing as $0 or NaN, missing currency, price in description not matching the pricing section
4. PROFESSIONAL: Client name missing or wrong, company name empty, broken email links

Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no explanation:
{"pass": true, "issues": [], "severity": "none"}
OR if issues found:
{"pass": false, "issues": ["description of issue 1"], "severity": "minor|major"}

Only FAIL for issues that would look unprofessional to a paying client.

IM
Iván Manfredi
Proposal • April 27, 2026
AI & Automation Architect

Personal AI Assistant

One WhatsApp number, one AI, one brain. You text or voice-note anything and it sorts itself.

27
APRIL 2026
Prepared ForCharles Cukier
EngagementPersonal
Delivery2 Weeks
Ownership100% Yours
01 • The Problem

What’s costing you time

You're juggling people, properties, follow-ups, research, and HubSpot busywork across too many tools. The cost isn't time so much as cognitive load: ideas dropped in the car, follow-ups slipping past their due date, twenty minutes of HubSpot setup every time you want to run a sequence, and a list of properties, grants, and market questions you've asked yourself to look into that never quite gets answered.

Currently absorbing approximately 15+ hours per week in manual work, with the additional cost of dropped follow-ups and missed context.

02 • The Solution

What we’re building

Random thoughts, reminders, research requests, PDFs to process, HubSpot jobs - everything lands in one chat. The brain figures out what you want, picks the right tool, and replies. No bot teams. No routing mazes. No picking which app to open.

Built on Claude + n8n, integrated with WhatsApp, HubSpot, Google Calendar, Gmail, Fireflies, Google Drive. Designed to remove manual touchpoints while keeping you in full control.

System Flow

01
Triggers
Three ways the assistant kicks in. (1) Inbound: you text or voice-note the WhatsApp number any time, in English or French. (2) Scheduled: cron jobs fire on their own - daily 7 AM digest, Monday chase digest, reminders firing at their set time, hourly Gmail and HubSpot polls. (3) Async events: research memos finishing, a chased reply landing, the brain pings you when something is ready.
02
Process
The brain reads or transcribes your message, figures out intent (capture, reminder, research, HubSpot job, pre-meeting brief), pulls memory of relevant people and properties, and picks the right tool from its toolbox.
03
Output
The answer comes back in WhatsApp, in whichever language you texted in. Reminders fire on time with the why attached. Memos arrive when ready. Briefs return in seconds. HubSpot jobs are prepared and shown to you before they run.
04
Confirm
Anything that sends a message, creates a record, or touches HubSpot waits for your explicit yes. No outbound action without confirmation, ever.

Stack

Built with
  • AI brain — Claude (by Anthropic, the same family powering most serious AI assistants today)
  • Workflow automation and scheduled jobs — n8n
  • WhatsApp infrastructure on a new dedicated number — Whapi
  • Voice-note transcription, sub-second turnaround — Groq Whisper
  • Your private memory database for people, properties, captures, follow-ups, and memos — Supabase
  • Property listing data from Centris and realtor.ca on a schedule — Apify
  • Web research and citation-backed lookups — Firecrawl + Perplexity Sonar Pro
Connects to
  • HubSpot — your CRM (contacts, tags, sequences)
  • Google Calendar — meetings for daily digest and pre-meeting briefs
  • Gmail — detects when chased replies land
  • Google Drive — read and write your documents (rent rolls, contracts, proformas)
  • Fireflies — meeting transcripts feed pre-meeting briefs and waiting-on items

Text 'walking to Evan' and get back 5 to 15 bullets in seconds - open follow-ups, recent activity, last meeting.

Live list of every chase, Monday digest of overdue items, auto-drafted follow-ups, tap-to-confirm closure.
05

7 AM brief: today's meetings with one-line context, overdue chases, research ready, yesterday's captures.
06

Contract → key-terms summary. Rent roll → proforma in a Google Sheet. Broker PDF → clean contacts ready for HubSpot.

06 • Expected ROI

What you get back

The real return is reclaimed cognitive load: nothing slips, nothing gets re-asked, follow-ups fire themselves, the list of properties and grants you've asked yourself to look into actually clears. On pure time, voice-note capture plus chat-ops HubSpot work alone returns a clear 10 to 15 hours a month. The compounding value is in deals you don't drop and ideas you don't lose.
